sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: My mother dead!
Re-enter HUBERT

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
HUBERT
KING JOHN
sentences.text: Five moons!

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Old men and beldams in the streets
Do prophesy upon it dangerously:
Young Arthur's death is common in their mouths:
And when they talk of him, they shake their heads
And whisper one another in the ear;
And he that speaks doth gripe the hearer's wrist,
Whilst he that hears makes fearful action,
With wrinkled brows, with nods, with rolling eyes.
I saw a smith stand with his hammer, thus,
The whilst his iron did on the anvil cool,
With open mouth swallowing a tailor's news;
Who, with his shears and measure in his hand,
Standing on slippers, which his nimble haste
Had falsely thrust upon contrary feet,
Told of a many thousand warlike French
That were embattailed and rank'd in Kent:
Another lean unwash'd artificer
Cuts off his tale and talks of Arthur's death.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Why seek'st thou to possess me with these fears?
Why urgest thou so oft young Arthur's death?
Thy hand hath murder'd him: I had a mighty cause
To wish him dead, but thou hadst none to kill him.

Why urgest thou so oft young Arthur's death?
Thy hand hath murder'd him: I had a mighty cause
To wish him dead, but thou hadst none to kill him.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: No had, my lord! why, did you not provoke me?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: No had, my lord! why, did you not provoke me?

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
It is the curse of kings to be attended
By slaves that take their humours for a warrant
To break within the bloody house of life,
And on the winking of authority
To understand a law, to know the meaning
Of dangerous majesty, when perchance it frowns
More upon humour than advised respect.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Here is your hand and seal for what I did.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
O, when the last account 'twixt heaven and earth
Is to be made, then shall this hand and seal
Witness against us to damnation!
How oft the sight of means to do ill deeds
Make deeds ill done! Hadst not thou been by,
A fellow by the hand of nature mark'd,
Quoted and sign'd to do a deed of shame,
This murder had not come into my mind:
But taking note of thy abhorr'd aspect,
Finding thee fit for bloody villany,
Apt, liable to be employ'd in danger,
I faintly broke with thee of Arthur's death;
And thou, to be endeared to a king,
Made it no conscience to destroy a prince.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: My lord—

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Hadst thou but shook thy head or made a pause
When I spake darkly what I purposed,
Or turn'd an eye of doubt upon my face,
As bid me tell my tale in express words,
Deep shame had struck me dumb, made me break off,
And those thy fears might have wrought fears in me:
But thou didst understand me by my signs
And didst in signs again parley with sin;
Yea, without stop, didst let thy heart consent,
And consequently thy rude hand to act
The deed, which both our tongues held vile to name.
Out of my sight, and never see me more!
My nobles leave me; and my state is braved,
Even at my gates, with ranks of foreign powers:
Nay, in the body of this fleshly land,
This kingdom, this confine of blood and breath,
Hostility and civil tumult reigns
Between my conscience and my cousin's death.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Arm you against your other enemies,
I'll make a peace between your soul and you.
Young Arthur is alive: this hand of mine
Is yet a maiden and an innocent hand,
Not painted with the crimson spots of blood.
Within this bosom never enter'd yet
The dreadful motion of a murderous thought;
And you have slander'd nature in my form,
Which, howsoever rude exteriorly,
Is yet the cover of a fairer mind
Than to be butcher of an innocent child.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Doth Arthur live? O, haste thee to the peers,
Throw this report on their incensed rage,
And make them tame to their obedience!
Forgive the comment that my passion made
Upon thy feature; for my rage was blind,
And foul imaginary eyes of blood
Presented thee more hideous than thou art.
O, answer not, but to my closet bring
The angry lords with all expedient haste.
I conjure thee but slowly; run more fast.

Enter ARTHUR, on the walls

sentences.speaker: ARTHUR
sentences.text:
The wall is high, and yet will I leap down:
Good ground, be pitiful and hurt me not!
There's few or none do know me: if they did,
This ship-boy's semblance hath disguised me quite.
I am afraid; and yet I'll venture it.
If I get down, and do not break my limbs,
I'll find a thousand shifts to get away:
As good to die and go, as die and stay.

O me! my uncle's spirit is in these stones:
Heaven take my soul, and England keep my bones!

Enter PEMBROKE, SALISBURY, and BIGOT

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Lords, I will meet him at Saint Edmundsbury:
It is our safety, and we must embrace
This gentle offer of the perilous time.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Lords, I will meet him at Saint Edmundsbury:
It is our safety, and we must embrace
This gentle offer of the perilous time.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text: Who brought that letter from the cardinal?

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
The Count Melun, a noble lord of France,
Whose private with me of the Dauphin's love
Is much more general than these lines import.

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text: To-morrow morning let us meet him then.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Or rather then set forward; for 'twill be
Two long days' journey, lords, or ere we meet.
Enter the BASTARD

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Once more to-day well met, distemper'd lords!
The king by me requests your presence straight.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Once more to-day well met, distemper'd lords!
The king by me requests your presence straight.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
The king hath dispossess'd himself of us:
We will not line his thin bestained cloak
With our pure honours, nor attend the foot
That leaves the print of blood where'er it walks.
Return and tell him so: we know the worst.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Whate'er you think, good words, I think, were best.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Our griefs, and not our manners, reason now.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
But there is little reason in your grief;
Therefore 'twere reason you had manners now.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text: Sir, sir, impatience hath his privilege.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: 'Tis true, to hurt his master, no man else.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: This is the prison. What is he lies here?

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: This is the prison. What is he lies here?

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: This is the prison. What is he lies here?

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
O death, made proud with pure and princely beauty!
The earth had not a hole to hide this deed.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
O death, made proud with pure and princely beauty!
The earth had not a hole to hide this deed.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Murder, as hating what himself hath done,
Doth lay it open to urge on revenge.

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text:
Or, when he doom'd this beauty to a grave,
Found it too precious-princely for a grave.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Sir Richard, what think you? have you beheld,
Or have you read or heard? or could you think?
Or do you almost think, although you see,
That you do see? could thought, without this object,
Form such another? This is the very top,
The height, the crest, or crest unto the crest,
Of murder's arms: this is the bloodiest shame,
The wildest savagery, the vilest stroke,
That ever wall-eyed wrath or staring rage
Presented to the tears of soft remorse.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
All murders past do stand excused in this:
And this, so sole and so unmatchable,
Shall give a holiness, a purity,
To the yet unbegotten sin of times;
And prove a deadly bloodshed but a jest,
Exampled by this heinous spectacle.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
SALISBURY
sentences.text:
If that it be the work of any hand!
We had a kind of light what would ensue:
It is the shameful work of Hubert's hand;
The practise and the purpose of the king:
From whose obedience I forbid my soul,
Kneeling before this ruin of sweet life,
And breathing to his breathless excellence
The incense of a vow, a holy vow,
Never to taste the pleasures of the world,
Never to be infected with delight,
Nor conversant with ease and idleness,
Till I have set a glory to this hand,
By giving it the worship of revenge .

By giving it the worship of revenge .

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
If that it be the work of any hand!
We had a kind of light what would ensue:
It is the shameful work of Hubert's hand;
The practise and the purpose of the king:
From whose obedience I forbid my soul,
Kneeling before this ruin of sweet life,
And breathing to his breathless excellence
The incense of a vow, a holy vow,
Never to taste the pleasures of the world,
Never to be infected with delight,
Nor conversant with ease and idleness,
Till I have set a glory to this hand,
By giving it the worship of revenge .

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text: Our souls religiously confirm thy words.
Enter HUBERT

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Lords, I am hot with haste in seeking you:
Arthur doth live; the king hath sent for you.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
O, he is old and blushes not at death.
Avaunt, thou hateful villain, get thee gone!

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
O, he is old and blushes not at death.
Avaunt, thou hateful villain, get thee gone!

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: I am no villain.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Must I rob the law?

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Must I rob the law?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Your sword is bright, sir; put it up again.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Your sword is bright, sir; put it up again.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Not till I sheathe it in a murderer's skin.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
HUBERT
HUBERT
HUBERT
BIGOT
sentences.text: Out, dunghill! darest thou brave a nobleman?

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text: Out, dunghill! darest thou brave a nobleman?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Not for my life: but yet I dare defend
My innocent life against an emperor.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Thou art a murderer.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
HUBERT
HUBERT
HUBERT
PEMBROKE
sentences.text: Cut him to pieces.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Keep the peace, I say.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Stand by, or I shall gall you, Faulconbridge.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Thou wert better gall the devil, Salisbury:
If thou but frown on me, or stir thy foot,
Or teach thy hasty spleen to do me shame,
I'll strike thee dead. Put up thy sword betime;
Or I'll so maul you and your toasting-iron,
That you shall think the devil is come from hell.

What wilt thou do, renowned Faulconbridge?
Second a villain and a murderer?

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text:
What wilt thou do, renowned Faulconbridge?
Second a villain and a murderer?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Lord Bigot, I am none.

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text: Who kill'd this prince?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
'Tis not an hour since I left him well:
I honour'd him, I loved him, and will weep
My date of life out for his sweet life's loss.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
'Tis not an hour since I left him well:
I honour'd him, I loved him, and will weep
My date of life out for his sweet life's loss.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Trust not those cunning waters of his eyes,
For villany is not without such rheum;
And he, long traded in it, makes it seem
Like rivers of remorse and innocency.
Away with me, all you whose souls abhor
The uncleanly savours of a slaughter-house;
For I am stifled with this smell of sin.

sentences.speaker: BIGOT
sentences.text: Away toward Bury, to the Dauphin there!

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text: There tell the king he may inquire us out.
Exeunt Lords

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Here's a good world! Knew you of this fair work?
Beyond the infinite and boundless reach
Of mercy, if thou didst this deed of death,
Art thou damn'd, Hubert.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Do but hear me, sir.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
BASTARD
BASTARD
BASTARD
HUBERT
sentences.text: Upon my soul—

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
If thou didst but consent
To this most cruel act, do but despair;
And if thou want'st a cord, the smallest thread
That ever spider twisted from her womb
Will serve to strangle thee, a rush will be a beam
To hang thee on; or wouldst thou drown thyself,
Put but a little water in a spoon,
And it shall be as all the ocean,
Enough to stifle such a villain up.
I do suspect thee very grievously.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
If I in act, consent, or sin of thought,
Be guilty of the stealing that sweet breath
Which was embounded in this beauteous clay,
Let hell want pains enough to torture me.
I left him well.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
If I in act, consent, or sin of thought,
Be guilty of the stealing that sweet breath
Which was embounded in this beauteous clay,
Let hell want pains enough to torture me.
I left him well.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Go, bear him in thine arms.
I am amazed, methinks, and lose my way
Among the thorns and dangers of this world.
How easy dost thou take all England up!
From forth this morsel of dead royalty,
The life, the right and truth of all this realm
Is fled to heaven; and England now is left
To tug and scamble and to part by the teeth
The unowed interest of proud-swelling state.
Now for the bare-pick'd bone of majesty
Doth dogged war bristle his angry crest
And snarleth in the gentle eyes of peace:
Now powers from home and discontents at home
Meet in one line; and vast confusion waits,
As doth a raven on a sick-fall'n beast,
The imminent decay of wrested pomp.
Now happy he whose cloak and cincture can
Hold out this tempest. Bear away that child
And follow me with speed: I'll to the king:
A thousand businesses are brief in hand,
And heaven itself doth frown upon the land.

Enter KING JOHN, CARDINAL PANDULPH, and Attendants

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Thus have I yielded up into your hand
The circle of my glory.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Thus have I yielded up into your hand
The circle of my glory.

sentences.speaker: CARDINAL PANDULPH
sentences.text:
Take again
From this my hand, as holding of the pope
Your sovereign greatness and authority.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Now keep your holy word: go meet the French,
And from his holiness use all your power
To stop their marches 'fore we are inflamed.
Our discontented counties do revolt;
Our people quarrel with obedience,
Swearing allegiance and the love of soul
To stranger blood, to foreign royalty.
This inundation of mistemper'd humour
Rests by you only to be qualified:
Then pause not; for the present time's so sick,
That present medicine must be minister'd,
Or overthrow incurable ensues.

sentences.speaker: CARDINAL PANDULPH
sentences.text:
It was my breath that blew this tempest up,
Upon your stubborn usage of the pope;
But since you are a gentle convertite,
My tongue shall hush again this storm of war
And make fair weather in your blustering land.
On this Ascension-day, remember well,
Upon your oath of service to the pope,
Go I to make the French lay down their arms.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Is this Ascension-day? Did not the prophet
Say that before Ascension-day at noon
My crown I should give off? Even so I have:
I did suppose it should be on constraint:
But, heaven be thank'd, it is but voluntary.

Enter the BASTARD

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
All Kent hath yielded; nothing there holds out
But Dover castle: London hath received,
Like a kind host, the Dauphin and his powers:
Your nobles will not hear you, but are gone
To offer service to your enemy,
And wild amazement hurries up and down
The little number of your doubtful friends.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Would not my lords return to me again,
After they heard young Arthur was alive?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
They found him dead and cast into the streets,
An empty casket, where the jewel of life
By some damn'd hand was robb'd and ta'en away.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: That villain Hubert told me he did live.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
So, on my soul, he did, for aught he knew.
But wherefore do you droop? why look you sad?
Be great in act, as you have been in thought;
Let not the world see fear and sad distrust
Govern the motion of a kingly eye:
Be stirring as the time; be fire with fire;
Threaten the threatener and outface the brow
Of bragging horror: so shall inferior eyes,
That borrow their behaviors from the great,
Grow great by your example and put on
The dauntless spirit of resolution.
Away, and glister like the god of war,
When he intendeth to become the field:
Show boldness and aspiring confidence.
What, shall they seek the lion in his den,
And fright him there? and make him tremble there?
O, let it not be said: forage, and run
To meet displeasure farther from the doors,
And grapple with him ere he comes so nigh.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
The legate of the pope hath been with me,
And I have made a happy peace with him;
And he hath promised to dismiss the powers
Led by the Dauphin.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
The legate of the pope hath been with me,
And I have made a happy peace with him;
And he hath promised to dismiss the powers
Led by the Dauphin.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
O inglorious league!
Shall we, upon the footing of our land,
Send fair-play orders and make compromise,
Insinuation, parley and base truce
To arms invasive? shall a beardless boy,
A cocker'd silken wanton, brave our fields,
And flesh his spirit in a warlike soil,
Mocking the air with colours idly spread,
And find no cheque? Let us, my liege, to arms:
Perchance the cardinal cannot make your peace;
Or if he do, let it at least be said
They saw we had a purpose of defence.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: Have thou the ordering of this present time.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Away, then, with good courage! yet, I know,
Our party may well meet a prouder foe.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Away, then, with good courage! yet, I know,
Our party may well meet a prouder foe.
Exeunt
Enter, in arms, LEWIS, SALISBURY, MELUN, PEMBROKE, BIGOT, and Soldiers

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
My Lord Melun, let this be copied out,
And keep it safe for our remembrance:
Return the precedent to these lords again;
That, having our fair order written down,
Both they and we, perusing o'er these notes,
May know wherefore we took the sacrament
And keep our faiths firm and inviolable.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
My Lord Melun, let this be copied out,

May know wherefore we took the sacrament
And keep our faiths firm and inviolable.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Upon our sides it never shall be broken.
And, noble Dauphin, albeit we swear
A voluntary zeal and an unurged faith
To your proceedings; yet believe me, prince,
I am not glad that such a sore of time
Should seek a plaster by contemn'd revolt,
And heal the inveterate canker of one wound
By making many. O, it grieves my soul,
That I must draw this metal from my side
To be a widow-maker! O, and there
Where honourable rescue and defence
Cries out upon the name of Salisbury!
But such is the infection of the time,
That, for the health and physic of our right,
We cannot deal but with the very hand
Of stern injustice and confused wrong.
And is't not pity, O my grieved friends,
That we, the sons and children of this isle,
Were born to see so sad an hour as this;
Wherein we step after a stranger march
Upon her gentle bosom, and fill up
Her enemies' ranks,—I must withdraw and weep
Upon the spot of this enforced cause,—
To grace the gentry of a land remote,
And follow unacquainted colours here?
What, here? O nation, that thou couldst remove!
That Neptune's arms, who clippeth thee about,
Would bear thee from the knowledge of thyself,
And grapple thee unto a pagan shore;
Where these two Christian armies might combine
The blood of malice in a vein of league,
And not to spend it so unneighbourly!

That Neptune's arms, who clippeth thee about,
Would bear thee from the knowledge of thyself,
And grapple thee unto a pagan shore;
Where these two Christian armies might combine
The blood of malice in a vein of league,
And not to spend it so unneighbourly!

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Upon our sides it never shall be broken.
And, noble Dauphin, albeit we swear
A voluntary zeal and an unurged faith
To your proceedings; yet believe me, prince,
I am not glad that such a sore of time
Should seek a plaster by contemn'd revolt,
And heal the inveterate canker of one wound
By making many. O, it grieves my soul,
That I must draw this metal from my side
To be a widow-maker! O, and there
Where honourable rescue and defence
Cries out upon the name of Salisbury!
But such is the infection of the time,
That, for the health and physic of our right,
We cannot deal but with the very hand
Of stern injustice and confused wrong.
And is't not pity, O my grieved friends,
That we, the sons and children of this isle,
Were born to see so sad an hour as this;
Wherein we step after a stranger march
Upon her gentle bosom, and fill up
Her enemies' ranks,—I must withdraw and weep
Upon the spot of this enforced cause,—
To grace the gentry of a land remote,
And follow unacquainted colours here?
What, here? O nation, that thou couldst remove!
That Neptune's arms, who clippeth thee about,
Would bear thee from the knowledge of thyself,
And grapple thee unto a pagan shore;
Where these two Christian armies might combine
The blood of malice in a vein of league,
And not to spend it so unneighbourly!

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
A noble temper dost thou show in this;
And great affections wrestling in thy bosom
Doth make an earthquake of nobility.
O, what a noble combat hast thou fought
Between compulsion and a brave respect!
Let me wipe off this honourable dew,
That silverly doth progress on thy cheeks:
My heart hath melted at a lady's tears,
Being an ordinary inundation;
But this effusion of such manly drops,
This shower, blown up by tempest of the soul,
Startles mine eyes, and makes me more amazed
Than had I seen the vaulty top of heaven
Figured quite o'er with burning meteors.
Lift up thy brow, renowned Salisbury,
And with a great heart heave away the storm:
Commend these waters to those baby eyes
That never saw the giant world enraged;
Nor met with fortune other than at feasts,
Full of warm blood, of mirth, of gossiping.
Come, come; for thou shalt thrust thy hand as deep
Into the purse of rich prosperity
As Lewis himself: so, nobles, shall you all,
That knit your sinews to the strength of mine.
And even there, methinks, an angel spake:

Enter CARDINAL PANDULPH

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Look, where the holy legate comes apace,
To give us warrant from the hand of heaven
And on our actions set the name of right
With holy breath.

sentences.speaker: CARDINAL PANDULPH
sentences.text:
Hail, noble prince of France!
The next is this, King John hath reconciled
Himself to Rome; his spirit is come in,
That so stood out against the holy church,
The great metropolis and see of Rome:
Therefore thy threatening colours now wind up;
And tame the savage spirit of wild war,
That like a lion foster'd up at hand,
It may lie gently at the foot of peace,
And be no further harmful than in show.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Your grace shall pardon me, I will not back:
I am too high-born to be propertied,
To be a secondary at control,
Or useful serving-man and instrument,
To any sovereign state throughout the world.
Your breath first kindled the dead coal of wars
Between this chastised kingdom and myself,
And brought in matter that should feed this fire;
And now 'tis far too huge to be blown out
With that same weak wind which enkindled it.
You taught me how to know the face of right,
Acquainted me with interest to this land,
Yea, thrust this enterprise into my heart;
And come ye now to tell me John hath made
His peace with Rome? What is that peace to me?
I, by the honour of my marriage-bed,
After young Arthur, claim this land for mine;
And, now it is half-conquer'd, must I back
Because that John hath made his peace with Rome?
Am I Rome's slave? What penny hath Rome borne,
What men provided, what munition sent,
To underprop this action? Is't not I
That undergo this charge? who else but I,
And such as to my claim are liable,
Sweat in this business and maintain this war?
Have I not heard these islanders shout out
'Vive le roi!' as I have bank'd their towns?
Have I not here the best cards for the game,
To win this easy match play'd for a crown?
And shall I now give o'er the yielded set?
No, no, on my soul, it never shall be said.

And such as to my claim are liable,
Sweat in this business and maintain this war?
Have I not heard these islanders shout out
'Vive le roi!' as I have bank'd their towns?
Have I not here the best cards for the game,
To win this easy match play'd for a crown?
And shall I now give o'er the yielded set?
No, no, on my soul, it never shall be said.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Your grace shall pardon me, I will not back:
I am too high-born to be propertied,
To be a secondary at control,
Or useful serving-man and instrument,
To any sovereign state throughout the world.
Your breath first kindled the dead coal of wars
Between this chastised kingdom and myself,
And brought in matter that should feed this fire;
And now 'tis far too huge to be blown out
With that same weak wind which enkindled it.
You taught me how to know the face of right,
Acquainted me with interest to this land,
Yea, thrust this enterprise into my heart;
And come ye now to tell me John hath made
His peace with Rome? What is that peace to me?
I, by the honour of my marriage-bed,
After young Arthur, claim this land for mine;
And, now it is half-conquer'd, must I back
Because that John hath made his peace with Rome?
Am I Rome's slave? What penny hath Rome borne,
What men provided, what munition sent,
To underprop this action? Is't not I
That undergo this charge? who else but I,
And such as to my claim are liable,
Sweat in this business and maintain this war?

sentences.speaker: CARDINAL PANDULPH
sentences.text: You look but on the outside of this work.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Outside or inside, I will not return
Till my attempt so much be glorified
As to my ample hope was promised
Before I drew this gallant head of war,
And cull'd these fiery spirits from the world,
To outlook conquest and to win renown
Even in the jaws of danger and of death.

Trumpet sounds

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text: What lusty trumpet thus doth summon us?

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text: What lusty trumpet thus doth summon us?
Enter the BASTARD, attended

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
According to the fair play of the world,
Let me have audience; I am sent to speak:
My holy lord of Milan, from the king
I come, to learn how you have dealt for him;
And, as you answer, I do know the scope
And warrant limited unto my tongue.

sentences.speaker: CARDINAL PANDULPH
CARDINAL PANDULPH
BASTARD
sentences.text:
By all the blood that ever fury breathed,
The youth says well. Now hear our English king;
For thus his royalty doth speak in me.
He is prepared, and reason too he should:
This apish and unmannerly approach,
This harness'd masque and unadvised revel,
This unhair'd sauciness and boyish troops,
The king doth smile at; and is well prepared
To whip this dwarfish war, these pigmy arms,
From out the circle of his territories.
That hand which had the strength, even at your door,
To cudgel you and make you take the hatch,
To dive like buckets in concealed wells,
To crouch in litter of your stable planks,
To lie like pawns lock'd up in chests and trunks,
To hug with swine, to seek sweet safety out
In vaults and prisons, and to thrill and shake
Even at the crying of your nation's crow,
Thinking his voice an armed Englishman;
Shall that victorious hand be feebled here,
That in your chambers gave you chastisement?
No: know the gallant monarch is in arms
And like an eagle o'er his aery towers,
To souse annoyance that comes near his nest.
And you degenerate, you ingrate revolts,
You bloody Neroes, ripping up the womb
Of your dear mother England, blush for shame;
For your own ladies and pale-visaged maids
Like Amazons come tripping after drums,
Their thimbles into armed gauntlets change,
Their needles to lances, and their gentle hearts
To fierce and bloody inclination.

Their needles to lances, and their gentle hearts
To fierce and bloody inclination.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
By all the blood that ever fury breathed,
The youth says well. Now hear our English king;
For thus his royalty doth speak in me.
He is prepared, and reason too he should:
This apish and unmannerly approach,
This harness'd masque and unadvised revel,
This unhair'd sauciness and boyish troops,
The king doth smile at; and is well prepared
To whip this dwarfish war, these pigmy arms,
From out the circle of his territories.
That hand which had the strength, even at your door,
To cudgel you and make you take the hatch,
To dive like buckets in concealed wells,
To crouch in litter of your stable planks,
To lie like pawns lock'd up in chests and trunks,
To hug with swine, to seek sweet safety out
In vaults and prisons, and to thrill and shake
Even at the crying of your nation's crow,
Thinking his voice an armed Englishman;
Shall that victorious hand be feebled here,
That in your chambers gave you chastisement?
No: know the gallant monarch is in arms
And like an eagle o'er his aery towers,
To souse annoyance that comes near his nest.
And you degenerate, you ingrate revolts,
You bloody Neroes, ripping up the womb
Of your dear mother England, blush for shame;
For your own ladies and pale-visaged maids
Like Amazons come tripping after drums,
Their thimbles into armed gauntlets change,
Their needles to lances, and their gentle hearts
To fierce and bloody inclination.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
LEWIS
LEWIS
LEWIS
CARDINAL PANDULPH
sentences.text: Give me leave to speak.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: No, I will speak.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
We will attend to neither.
Strike up the drums; and let the tongue of war
Plead for our interest and our being here.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
We will attend to neither.
Strike up the drums; and let the tongue of war
Plead for our interest and our being here.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Indeed your drums, being beaten, will cry out;
And so shall you, being beaten: do but start
An echo with the clamour of thy drum,
And even at hand a drum is ready braced
That shall reverberate all as loud as thine;
Sound but another, and another shall
As loud as thine rattle the welkin's ear
And mock the deep-mouth'd thunder: for at hand,
Not trusting to this halting legate here,
Whom he hath used rather for sport than need
Is warlike John; and in his forehead sits
A bare-ribb'd death, whose office is this day
To feast upon whole thousands of the French.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text: Strike up our drums, to find this danger out.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: And thou shalt find it, Dauphin, do not doubt.
Exeunt
Alarums. Enter KING JOHN and HUBERT
Alarums. Enter KING JOHN and HUBERT

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: How goes the day with us? O, tell me, Hubert.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: How goes the day with us? O, tell me, Hubert.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Badly, I fear. How fares your majesty?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Badly, I fear. How fares your majesty?

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
This fever, that hath troubled me so long,
Lies heavy on me; O, my heart is sick!

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
This fever, that hath troubled me so long,
Lies heavy on me; O, my heart is sick!
Enter a Messenger

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
My lord, your valiant kinsman, Faulconbridge,
Desires your majesty to leave the field
And send him word by me which way you go.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text: Tell him, toward Swinstead, to the abbey there.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Be of good comfort; for the great supply
That was expected by the Dauphin here,
Are wreck'd three nights ago on Goodwin Sands.
This news was brought to Richard but even now:
The French fight coldly, and retire themselves.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Be of good comfort; for the great supply
That was expected by the Dauphin here,
Are wreck'd three nights ago on Goodwin Sands.
This news was brought to Richard but even now:
The French fight coldly, and retire themselves.

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Ay me! this tyrant fever burns me up,
And will not let me welcome this good news.
Set on toward Swinstead: to my litter straight;
Weakness possesseth me, and I am faint.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: I did not think the king so stored with friends.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
Up once again; put spirit in the French:
If they miscarry, we miscarry too.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
Up once again; put spirit in the French:
If they miscarry, we miscarry too.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
Up once again; put spirit in the French:
If they miscarry, we miscarry too.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
That misbegotten devil, Faulconbridge,
In spite of spite, alone upholds the day.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text: They say King John sore sick hath left the field.
Enter MELUN, wounded

sentences.speaker: MELUN
sentences.text: Lead me to the revolts of England here.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: When we were happy we had other names.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text: It is the Count Melun.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: Wounded to death.

sentences.speaker: MELUN
sentences.text:
Fly, noble English, you are bought and sold;
Unthread the rude eye of rebellion
And welcome home again discarded faith.
Seek out King John and fall before his feet;
For if the French be lords of this loud day,
He means to recompense the pains you take
By cutting off your heads: thus hath he sworn
And I with him, and many moe with me,
Upon the altar at Saint Edmundsbury;
Even on that altar where we swore to you
Dear amity and everlasting love.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: May this be possible? may this be true?

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text: May this be possible? may this be true?

sentences.speaker: MELUN
sentences.text:
Have I not hideous death within my view,
Retaining but a quantity of life,
Which bleeds away, even as a form of wax
Resolveth from his figure 'gainst the fire?
What in the world should make me now deceive,
Since I must lose the use of all deceit?
Why should I then be false, since it is true
That I must die here and live hence by truth?
I say again, if Lewis do win the day,
He is forsworn, if e'er those eyes of yours
Behold another day break in the east:
But even this night, whose black contagious breath
Already smokes about the burning crest
Of the old, feeble and day-wearied sun,
Even this ill night, your breathing shall expire,
Paying the fine of rated treachery
Even with a treacherous fine of all your lives,
If Lewis by your assistance win the day.
Commend me to one Hubert with your king:
The love of him, and this respect besides,
For that my grandsire was an Englishman,
Awakes my conscience to confess all this.
In lieu whereof, I pray you, bear me hence
From forth the noise and rumour of the field,
Where I may think the remnant of my thoughts
In peace, and part this body and my soul
With contemplation and devout desires.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
We do believe thee: and beshrew my soul
But I do love the favour and the form
Of this most fair occasion, by the which
We will untread the steps of damned flight,
And like a bated and retired flood,
Leaving our rankness and irregular course,
Stoop low within those bounds we have o'erlook'd
And cabby run on in obedience
Even to our ocean, to our great King John.
My arm shall give thee help to bear thee hence;
For I do see the cruel pangs of death
Right in thine eye. Away, my friends! New flight;
And happy newness, that intends old righ t.

We do believe thee: and beshrew my soul
But I do love the favour and the form
Of this most fair occasion, by the which
We will untread the steps of damned flight,
And like a bated and retired flood,
Leaving our rankness and irregular course,
Stoop low within those bounds we have o'erlook'd
And cabby run on in obedience
Even to our ocean, to our great King John.
My arm shall give thee help to bear thee hence;
For I do see the cruel pangs of death
Right in thine eye. Away, my friends! New flight;
And happy newness, that intends old righ t.

Exeunt, leading off MELUN
Enter LEWIS and his train

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
The sun of heaven methought was loath to set,
But stay'd and made the western welkin blush,
When English measure backward their own ground
In faint retire. O, bravely came we off,
When with a volley of our needless shot,
After such bloody toil, we bid good night;
And wound our tattering colours clearly up,
Last in the field, and almost lords of it!

Enter a Messenger

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Where is my prince, the Dauphin?

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text: Here: what news?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
The Count Melun is slain; the English lords
By his persuasion are again fall'n off,
And your supply, which you have wish'd so long,
Are cast away and sunk on Goodwin Sands.

Are cast away and sunk on Goodwin Sands.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Ah, foul shrewd news! beshrew thy very heart!
I did not think to be so sad to-night
As this hath made me. Who was he that said
King John did fly an hour or two before
The stumbling night did part our weary powers?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Whoever spoke it, it is true, my lord.

sentences.speaker: LEWIS
sentences.text:
Well; keep good quarter and good care to-night:
The day shall not be up so soon as I,
To try the fair adventure of to-morrow.

Enter the BASTARD and HUBERT, severally

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Who's there? speak, ho! speak quickly, or I shoot.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Who's there? speak, ho! speak quickly, or I shoot.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Who's there? speak, ho! speak quickly, or I shoot.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: A friend. What art thou?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: A friend. What art thou?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text: Of the part of England.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Whither dost thou go?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
What's that to thee? why may not I demand
Of thine affairs, as well as thou of mine?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
What's that to thee? why may not I demand
Of thine affairs, as well as thou of mine?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Hubert, I think?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Thou hast a perfect thought:
I will upon all hazards well believe
Thou art my friend, that know'st my tongue so well.
Who art thou?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Thou hast a perfect thought:
I will upon all hazards well believe
Thou art my friend, that know'st my tongue so well.
Who art thou?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Thou hast a perfect thought:
I will upon all hazards well believe
Thou art my friend, that know'st my tongue so well.
Who art thou?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Who thou wilt: and if thou please,
Thou mayst befriend me so much as to think
I come one way of the Plantagenets.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Unkind remembrance! thou and eyeless night
Have done me shame: brave soldier, pardon me,
That any accent breaking from thy tongue
Should 'scape the true acquaintance of mine ear.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:

Have done me shame: brave soldier, pardon me,

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Come, come; sans compliment, what news abroad?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Come, come; sans compliment, what news abroad?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Why, here walk I in the black brow of night,
To find you out.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Brief, then; and what's the news?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
O, my sweet sir, news fitting to the night,
Black, fearful, comfortless and horrible.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Show me the very wound of this ill news:
I am no woman, I'll not swoon at it.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Show me the very wound of this ill news:
I am no woman, I'll not swoon at it.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
The king, I fear, is poison'd by a monk:
I left him almost speechless; and broke out
To acquaint you with this evil, that you might
The better arm you to the sudden time,
Than if you had at leisure known of this.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: How did he take it? who did taste to him?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: How did he take it? who did taste to him?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
A monk, I tell you; a resolved villain,
Whose bowels suddenly burst out: the king
Yet speaks and peradventure may recover.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:

Whose bowels suddenly burst out: the king
Yet speaks and peradventure may recover.

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
A monk, I tell you; a resolved villain,
Whose bowels suddenly burst out: the king
Yet speaks and peradventure may recover.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text: Who didst thou leave to tend his majesty?

sentences.speaker: HUBERT
sentences.text:
Why, know you not? the lords are all come back,
And brought Prince Henry in their company;
At whose request the king hath pardon'd them,
And they are all about his majesty.

sentences.text: Exeunt
Enter PRINCE HENRY, SALISBURY, and BIGOT

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
It is too late: the life of all his blood
Is touch'd corruptibly, and his pure brain,
Which some suppose the soul's frail dwelling-house,
Doth by the idle comments that it makes
Foretell the ending of mortality.

Enter PEMBROKE

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
His highness yet doth speak, and holds belief
That, being brought into the open air,
It would allay the burning quality
Of that fell poison which assaileth him.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Let him be brought into the orchard here.
Doth he still rage?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Let him be brought into the orchard here.
Doth he still rage?
Exit BIGOT

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
He is more patient
Than when you left him; even now he sung.

sentences.speaker: PEMBROKE
sentences.text:
He is more patient
Than when you left him; even now he sung.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
O vanity of sickness! fierce extremes
In their continuance will not feel themselves.
Death, having prey'd upon the outward parts,
Leaves them invisible, and his siege is now
Against the mind, the which he pricks and wounds
With many legions of strange fantasies,
Which, in their throng and press to that last hold,
Confound themselves. 'Tis strange that death
should sing.
I am the cygnet to this pale faint swan,
Who chants a doleful hymn to his own death,
And from the organ-pipe of frailty sings
His soul and body to their lasting rest.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Be of good comfort, prince; for you are born
To set a form upon that indigest
Which he hath left so shapeless and so rude.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:

Enter Attendants, and BIGOT, carrying KING JOHN in a chair

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Ay, marry, now my soul hath elbow-room;
It would not out at windows nor at doors.
There is so hot a summer in my bosom,
That all my bowels crumble up to dust:
I am a scribbled form, drawn with a pen
Upon a parchment, and against this fire
Do I shrink up.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text: How fares your majesty?

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
Poison'd,—ill fare—dead, forsook, cast off:
And none of you will bid the winter come
To thrust his icy fingers in my maw,
Nor let my kingdom's rivers take their course
Through my burn'd bosom, nor entreat the north
To make his bleak winds kiss my parched lips
And comfort me with cold. I do not ask you much,
I beg cold comfort; and you are so strait
And so ingrateful, you deny me that.

O that there were some virtue in my tears,
That might relieve you!

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
The salt in them is hot.
Within me is a hell; and there the poison
Is as a fiend confined to tyrannize
On unreprievable condemned blood.

Enter the BASTARD

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
O, I am scalded with my violent motion,
And spleen of speed to see your majesty!

sentences.speaker: KING JOHN
sentences.text:
O cousin, thou art come to set mine eye:
The tackle of my heart is crack'd and burn'd,
And all the shrouds wherewith my life should sail
Are turned to one thread, one little hair:
My heart hath one poor string to stay it by,
Which holds but till thy news be uttered;
And then all this thou seest is but a clod
And module of confounded royalty.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
The Dauphin is preparing hitherward,
Where heaven He knows how we shall answer him;
For in a night the best part of my power,
As I upon advantage did remove,
Were in the Washes all unwarily
Devoured by the unexpected flood.

KING JOHN dies

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
You breathe these dead news in as dead an ear.
My liege! my lord! but now a king, now thus.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:

My liege! my lord! but now a king, now thus.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Even so must I run on, and even so stop.
What surety of the world, what hope, what stay,
When this was now a king, and now is clay?

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
Even so must I run on, and even so stop.
What surety of the world, what hope, what stay,
When this was now a king, and now is clay?

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Art thou gone so? I do but stay behind
To do the office for thee of revenge,
And then my soul shall wait on thee to heaven,
As it on earth hath been thy servant still.
Now, now, you stars that move in your right spheres,
Where be your powers? show now your mended faiths,
And instantly return with me again,
To push destruction and perpetual shame
Out of the weak door of our fainting land.
Straight let us seek, or straight we shall be sought;
The Dauphin rages at our very heels.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
It seems you know not, then, so much as we:
The Cardinal Pandulph is within at rest,
Who half an hour since came from the Dauphin,
And brings from him such offers of our peace
As we with honour and respect may take,
With purpose presently to leave this wa r.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
He will the rather do it when he sees
Ourselves well sinewed to our defence.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
Nay, it is in a manner done already;
For many carriages he hath dispatch'd
To the sea-side, and put his cause and quarrel
To the disposing of the cardinal:
With whom yourself, myself and other lords,
If you think meet, this afternoon will post
To consummate this business happily.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:

To consummate this business happily.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Let it be so: and you, my noble prince,
With other princes that may best be spared,
Shall wait upon your father's funeral.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Let it be so: and you, my noble prince,
With other princes that may best be spared,
Shall wait upon your father's funeral.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
At Worcester must his body be interr'd;
For so he will'd it.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Thither shall it then:
And happily may your sweet self put on
The lineal state and glory of the land!
To whom with all submission, on my knee
I do bequeath my faithful services
And true subjection everlastingly.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
Thither shall it then:
And happily may your sweet self put on
The lineal state and glory of the land!
To whom with all submission, on my knee
I do bequeath my faithful services
And true subjection everlastingly.

sentences.speaker: SALISBURY
sentences.text:
And the like tender of our love we make,
To rest without a spot for evermore.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE HENRY
sentences.text:
I have a kind soul that would give you thanks
And knows not how to do it but with tears.

sentences.speaker: BASTARD
sentences.text:
O, let us pay the time but needful woe,
Since it hath been beforehand with our griefs.
This England never did, nor never shall,
Lie at the proud foot of a conqueror,
But when it first did help to wound itself.
Now these her princes are come home again,
Come the three corners of the world in arms,
And we shall shock them. Nought shall make us rue,
If England to itself do rest but true.
